California
Fair POlitical
Practices Cornmission
March 6, 1992
Malcol,m E. wheeler
Parcel, Mauro, Hultin & Spaanstra, P.c.
Suite 3600, 180I californj,a Street
Denver, Col-orado 80202-2636
Re: Your Request For Advi-ce
Our FiIe No. 92-86
Dear l,Ir. wheel-er:
You have requested advice under the lobbying provisions of
the Pol,itical Reform Act of 1974.1/
QUESTION
Are you required to attend the lobbyist orientation course
described in Governrnent Code Section 8956(b). which is a
requi-rement of the lobbyist certificatj-on provisions of Section
86I03?
CONCLUSION
If you do not intend to
California in the future, you
lobbyist orientation course.
engage in lobbying activity in
are not required to attend the
FACTS
You are currently a resident of Denver, col-orado, where you
practi.ce 1aw. For a brief period of time during 1991, you were
regj-stered as a lobbyist in california, and met with a member of
the Assenbly concerning a legislative biII. 
You terminated your
lobbyist registration in August 199I, and have not engaged in any
lobbying activity in Californi,a since that date. You do not
intend to engage in Iobbying activity in California in the future.
l/ 
covernnent code Sectj,ons 81000-91015. AIl statutory
references are to the Government Code unl-ess otherwise indicated.
comrnission regulations appear at 2 california code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. AII references to regulations are to
Ti-t]e 2, Division 6 of the california code of Regulations.
.128 J Strcct, Sr.ritc Strt) a I'j.(). lJox Sr,)7. o Sncrrrrrtntr) Cr\ 95S0+-Ol'i0; a (91()) lll-i6(.lLl


Malcoln E. wheeler
Page Two
ANALYSIS
Section 86103 provides that an individual who, at the time of
filing the lobbyist certification 
form, has not completed the
course described i.n Government code Section 8956(b) within the
previous 24 months, must complete the course within a reasonable
time after filing the certification papers. The Lobbyist
certification 
is considered "conditionaltt until the individual has
attended the course.
After consultation with Ann Bail-ey, Chief Counsel for the
senate Committee on Legislatj,ve Ethics, and Tony Marquez, Chief
Counsel, for the Joint Legislative Ethics Connittee, it has been
deterrnined that you are not required to attend the lobbyist
orientation course described in Section 86103. This deterni.nation
is based on the partj.cular facts of your situation, speci-ficaIly
that your only Lobbying activity consisted of neeting with a
legislator on one legislative bill during a brief period of tine
in 199I, that you have ceased all lobbying activity in California,
and that you do not intend to engage in lobbying activity in
California in the future.
we have sent a copy of this letter to the Secretary of
State's office, to serve as explanation for why you are not
required to amend your lobbyist certification.
If you have any questions, please call our office at (916)
322-5562 .
Sincerely,
Scott HaIlabrin
Acting GeneraXounsel
